channel.title: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: The Unlikely Alliance That Toppled Romania’s Government
item.description: Leftist and hard-right lawmakers passed a no-confidence motion ousting Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan.

item.title: America must hope Donald Trump is not a new Caligula
item.description: In the annals of rulers committing acts of folly, Roman decadence stands out

item.title: Can Bill Ackman save the closed-end fund?
item.description: An outspoken financier wants to build a modern-day Berkshire Hathaway

item.title: Britain Leaves Two-Party Politics Behind
item.description: Local elections this week will lay bare growing fragmentation across the United Kingdom.

item.title: The New Critical Minerals Map
item.description: Fears of Chinese and U.S. trade retaliation have spawned deals that exclude both powers.

item.title: How AI tools could enable bioterrorism
item.description: Leading models are getting better at designing pathogens

item.title: Amb. Ina Marciulionyte on the Future of EU-Mongolia Ties
item.description: EU projects “prioritize sustainable development, economic development, and democracy in Mongolia” – and, increasingly “peace and security” as well.

item.title: Livable Cities Are the Key to India’s Growth
item.description: India is urbanizing rapidly, but poor public services, weak local governance, and unregulated sprawl are impeding productivity growth. The solution lies in reforms that empower local governments, improve administrative efficiency, and strengthen coordination of city planning and infrastructure development.

item.title: Trump’s Gaza Impasse
item.description: Experts say Hamas has few incentives to disarm.

item.title: North Korea’s Strongwoman-in-Waiting
item.description: Kim Jong Un is orchestrating his daughter’s ascent through a campaign of maternal statecraft.

item.title: To combat antisemitism, first grasp where it comes from
item.description: What looks like a 21st-century problem has deep, dark roots

item.title: To fight antisemitism, first grasp where it comes from
item.description: What looks like a 21st-century problem has deep, dark roots

item.title: Four Scenarios for the Iran War
item.description: After failing to clinch a decisive victory against Iran, US President Donald Trump is now facing deteriorating macroeconomic conditions ahead of November's midterm elections. With his options limited, he has been left to choose between wishful thinking and a high-risk re-escalation of hostilities.

item.title: Japan’s Arms Export Shift Takes Shape in the Philippines
item.description: The plan to transfer warships to Manila reflects Tokyo’s new Indo-Pacific security role.

item.title: Will the US Serra Verde Acquisition Help Break China’s Rare Earth Monopoly?
item.description: One of the primary drivers behind the acquisition, backed by the U.S. government, is to disrupt the prevailing Chinese monopoly in rare earths. Will it actually work?

item.title: Better Late Than Never: Italy’s Strategic Bet on India
item.description: Italy’s growing engagement with India reflects a broader, even if somewhat belated, Indo-Pacific turn.

item.title: Mea culpa
item.description: Our report from 1974

item.title: Taiwan’s President Makes Surprise Trip to Eswatini
item.description: Lai Ching-te succeeded in making his visit to Taiwan’s lone ally in Africa just weeks after China forced a high-profile cancellation of his original itinerary.

item.title: The Return to Infrastructure-Led Growth
item.description: Given China's disappointing forays into consumption stimulus over the past few years, there can be little doubt that its recent strategic shift back toward infrastructure-driven growth is fully justified. All the talk among international commentators about overcapacity ignores the realities on the ground.

item.title: South Korea’s Ruling Party Has a Jung Chung-rae Risk
item.description: With President Lee Jae-myung riding high in the polls and the June 3 local elections looking like a sweep, the ruling Democratic Party’s leader keeps getting in the way.

item.title: Foreign-Invested Apps and Taiwan’s Cybersecurity Blind Spot
item.description: The data generated by millions of Taiwanese users – their movement patterns, consumption habits, and delivery addresses – is increasingly spread among numerous foreign investors, including companies from China.

item.title: Asia’s stranded seafarers suffer as the Iran war drags on
item.description: In a more dangerous world, unsung mariners are under increasing threat

item.title: Jerome Powell’s Finest Hour
item.description: Just as Winston Churchill is primarily remembered for facing down the Nazi onslaught in World War II, outgoing US Federal Reserve Jerome Powell is likely to be remembered for his courageous defense of the Fed’s independence against President Donald Trump’s attacks. Like Churchill, he will be forgiven his major policy errors.

item.title: Russia’s African Recruitment Web Is Expanding
item.description: Deceptive job schemes and transnational networks are pulling Kenyans into Moscow’s war.

item.title: Javier Milei is in serious trouble
item.description: Argentina’s president claims he is the true victim of a struggling economy

item.title: The War Room newsletter: Is Russia being out-droned?
item.description: Shashank Joshi, our defence editor, invites a Ukrainian drone expert to write a guest edition

item.title: Trump’s Southeast Asia Trade Deals Are in Limbo
item.description: Malaysia becomes the first country to cancel its tariff deal with the White House.

item.title: Accounting for Atrocity
item.description: When it comes to prosecuting a toppled regime’s killers, torturers, and enablers, a balance must be struck between justice and stability. Syria’s new leaders are realizing, like Charles de Gaulle and Konrad Adenauer before them, that they need the support of tainted elites to rebuild their broken societies.

item.title: Cover Story newsletter: Oil markets are still in La La land
item.description: How we chose this week’s image

item.title: BJP Makes Major Gains in 3 of 5 State Assembly Elections
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi's party has stormed to power in West Bengal and retained its hold over Assam and Puducherry.

item.title: The ICJ and the Limits of International Justice For the Rohingya
item.description: International courts can apportion legal responsibility, but cannot guarantee justice, safety, or safe return to Myanmar for more than 1 million Rohingya refugees.

item.title: Only Congress Can Fix American Trade
item.description: Trump’s tariff mess offers a chance to restore legislative oversight.

item.title: The Return of Japanese Hard Power
item.description: Why Tokyo is bulking up its defense-industrial base.

item.title: Trump’s China Trap
item.description: Why Xi keeps winning the summitry game.

item.title: Singapore, New Zealand Agree to Keep Vital Supplies Flowing in Times of Crisis
item.description: The Agreement on Trade in Essential Supplies, which was agreed in October, is the world’s first legally-binding bilateral supply chain resilience agreement.

item.title: Indonesia, Japan Sign New Defense Agreement, Eye Possible Arms Transfers
item.description: With the recent removal of its longstanding ban on lethal arms exports, Tokyo is set to become an active defense industry player in Southeast Asia.
